content.full_text:
Ruth Schneider
Mayor

FPPC
JUN 11 | '03 PM '90

Allison Ruedy
Councilmember

Kathleen Foote
Vice Mayor

Richard D. Spotswood
Councilmember

Warren Levinson
Councilmember

Doug Dawson
City Manager

MILL VALLEY

388-4033

June 7, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Sir or Madam:

While doing some research on fair campaigning, I came across this reference in a packet prepared by the City of Chico. Is there still a "Model Fair Campaign Practices Agreement" and if so, may I please obtain a copy?

Our city council has appointed an ad hoc citizens committee to explore the subject of ethics in local campaigning and thought this might prove helpful.

Thank you.

Sincerely,

Mary Herr
City Clerk

City of Mill Valley, P.O. Box 1029, California 94942

FAIR CAMPAIGN PRACTICES ACT

APRIL 12, 1983, GENERAL MUNICIPAL ELECTION


Each candidate is being provided with the following documents:

- Code of Fair Campaign Practices (provided by the Secretary of State)

- Provisions of the Code of Fair Campaign Practices (provided by the
Secretary of State)

- Model Fair Campaign Practices Agreement (provided by the Fair Political
Practices Commission)

- News Release from the City Council dated September 27, 1982, concerning
the Fair Political Practices Agreement

- Briefing Book on Misleading, Negative and Last Minute Campaign Advertising
(provided by the Fair Political Practices Commission)

It is voluntary on the part of each candidate to execute the Code of Fair
Campaign Practices and the Model Fair Campaign Practices Agreement, and return
them to the City Clerk. These will be kept on file in the City Clerk's Office.

At the close of the nomination period, the candidates executing these documents
may wish to hold a meeting for the purpose of discussing the provisions of the
Fair Campaign Practices Code and Agreement. If the candidates decide to select
an independent panel as mentioned in the Agreement, you will note in the City
Council's News Release it urged that the one additional member to be agreed upon
by the other panelists be someone associated with the Butte County League of
Women Voters.

Since all of the above is voluntary on the part of candidates, the City Clerk's
Office will not be involved in coordinating or scheduling any meetings and this
will be left entirely up to the candidates. Anyone may, however, call the City
Clerk's Office to reserve a meeting room in the Council Chamber Building at 425
Main Street for any of your meetings. Rooms in the Building are available to
all organizations and agencies and are taken on a first come, first served basis.

sections.question: Is there still a "Model Fair Campaign Practices Agreement" and if so, may I please obtain a copy?
sections.question_synthetic: The City Clerk of Mill Valley is inquiring whether the FPPC still maintains a Model Fair Campaign Practices Agreement and requests a copy for use by their city council's ethics committee.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. Contains reference material about Fair Campaign Practices from City of Chico but no Q/C sections typical of advice letters. The attached materials appear to be background documentation from an April 1983 municipal election rather than the FPPC's response.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming correspondence from the City Clerk of Mill Valley requesting information about the availability and a copy of the FPPC's Model Fair Campaign Practices Agreement for use by their city council's ethics committee.
